methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals intentional distribution of deep AI capabilities beyond NoVA through education infrastructure, creating potential peer learning networks that could spread fluency across Richmond's workforce rather than concentrating expertise.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded workforce training in Southwest Virginia demonstrates community-driven capacity building in an historically underserved region, suggesting distributed development pathways that could enable deep skills rather than platform dependency.

signals.title: Fortreum acquires Reston-based cybersecurity firm
signals.signal_reading: Fortreum's acquisition of Reston-based AI compliance startup Kovr.ai reinforces NoVA's concentration of specialized AI capability and capital flows, deepening technical expertise in the metro hub while other regions remain acquisition-absent.

signals.title: 6,000-acre innovation district launches in Manassas
signals.signal_reading: The 6,000-acre Manassas innovation district partnership with George Mason represents massive infrastructure investment concentrating advanced AI and tech capabilities in the NoVA corridor, creating a gravitational pull for talent and resources away from rural regions.

signals.title: Doug Hoven on How AI Is Changing the Way Homes Are Bought and Sold in Hampton Roads - The Ritz Herald
signals.signal_reading: AI integration in Hampton Roads real estate signals platform-delivered automation creating broad but shallow adoption—agents using AI tools without building deep technical capability, spreading access but not expertise.
